change_details: Floor advantage-std normalization at 0.2 via max(var, 0.04) — prevents late-training advantage-magnification spikes that drive policy collapse.
rationale: The policy loss divides by advantage std with only a 1e-6 epsilon guard. When the value head saturates (vAbs → 1.0) or a batch lands on uniform-outcome positions late in training, batch advantage variance can collapse by 100×+, magnifying per-row REINFORCE weights by the same factor. The global L2 clip flattens the resulting spike but cannot fully neutralize it: SGD step direction absorbs the outlier and pushes one logit toward saturation — exactly the illegal-mass-collapse signature. Replacing var+1e-6 with max(var, 0.04) (std floor 0.2) is a no-op in healthy regimes (z∈{−1,0,+1} mixed with v_baseline produces std ≈ 0.4–1.0) but engages precisely when collapse pressure rises. Orthogonal to entropy_bonus / weight_decay / lr_warmup_steps: this is a stability guard on the loss formulation itself, not reachable by tuning a parameter. graph.maximum is already used elsewhere (the gradient clip), so MPSGraph autograd handles the path.
